dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HRES 862 IH: Expressing the sense of the House of Representatives that a commemorative postage stamp should be issued in honor of the Buffalo Soldiers.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Beatty (for herself, Mr. Horsford, Mr. Fields, Ms. Kamlager-Dove, Mr. Cohen, Mr. Green of Texas, Mr. Bell, Mrs. McIver, Mr. Jackson of Illinois, and Mr. Veasey) submitted the following resolution; which was referred to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform

form.official-title: Expressing the sense of the House of Representatives that a commemorative postage stamp should be issued in honor of the Buffalo Soldiers.

whereas.text: Whereas, on July 28, 1866, Congress established 6 all-Black regiments, later consolidated to 4, to help rebuild the country after the Civil War and to patrol the remote western frontier during the Indian wars;
Whereas Colonel Charles Young was a Buffalo Soldier and the highest ranking African-American commanding officer in the United States Army from 1894 until his death in 1922;
Whereas more than 200,000 African Americans served in World War I and more than 1,000,000 served in World War II;
Whereas the Buffalo Soldiers received their name because of the buffalo’s fierce bravery and fighting spirit;
Whereas African-American troops accepted the name, Buffalo Soldiers, with pride and honor;
Whereas the Buffalo Soldiers fought alongside White regiments in many conflicts and were instrumental in the exploration and settlement of western lands;
Whereas, over the 82 years of the Buffalo Soldiers’ existence, 23 men received the Congressional Medal of Honor, the highest recognition awarded by the United States Government for military service;
Whereas the Buffalo Soldiers ceased to exist in 1948 when President Harry Truman signed Executive Order 9981 mandating equal treatment and opportunity for African-American servicemen;
Whereas the Buffalo Soldiers are a significant part of American military history;
Whereas a stamp was issued in honor of the Buffalo Soldiers on April 22, 1994, and this stamp was placed on off sale in December 1995; and
Whereas reissuing a postage stamp to honor the Buffalo Soldiers is fitting and proper: Now, therefore, be it

section.text: That it is the sense of the House of Representatives that—

paragraph.text: a commemorative postage stamp should be issued in honor of the Buffalo Soldiers; and
the Citizens’ Stamp Advisory Committee should recommend to the Postmaster General that such a stamp be issued.
